pattern: Fixing the first chunk's numeric value and then greedily requiring each following chunk to equal the previous value minus one turns the search into a bounded backtracking over the first split point.
hints: Leading zeros are allowed, so a chunk's value is just the integer it spells regardless of zeros.
Once the first piece's value is chosen, every later piece must be exactly one less than the piece before it.
Try every prefix as the first number, then recursively check whether the rest can be peeled off as value-1, value-2, and so on until the whole string is consumed with at least two pieces.

edge_inputs.description: equal pieces are not descending by one
long run down to single digits

stress.description: random short digit strings, mostly forcing backtracking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 20)
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: constructed valid descending sequences padded with zeros
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    start = rng.randint(1, 99)
    parts = []
    v = start
    s = ''
    while v >= 0 and len(s) < 18:
        pad = rng.randint(0, 1)
        chunk = ('0' * pad) + str(v)
        if len(s) + len(chunk) > 20:
            break
        s += chunk
        v -= 1
        if len(s) >= 2 and rng.random() < 0.3:
            break
    if not s:
        s = '10'
    return [s]
